revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Arioka Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Historic Kofuns in Zentsūji, Kagawa, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Arioka Kofun cluster | native_name =有岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohakayama_Kofun_(Zentsuji),_funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ohakayama_Kofun in the Arioka Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Arioka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|13|00|N|133|47|25|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji, Kagawa|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th - mid-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|13|00|N|133|47|25|E||qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Arioka Kofun Cluster''' |有岡古墳群|Arioka Kofun-gun||qq=}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt= Zentsūji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1984.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202756/3|title=有岡古墳群 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2021|qq=}}</ref> It is claimed to be a group of tombs of the powerful local family who were the ancestors of the famed prelate {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}. ==Overview== The Arioka Kofun cluster is located in the central part of Kagawa Prefecture, and are located on a hill in the southwestern part of Zentsūji city, overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. The site consists of six tumuli. The all are {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with the exception of the Miyagao Kofun, which is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}). Of these tumuli, four are protected by the National Historic Site designation and are well maintained. The '''Nota-no-in Kofun''' was built in the latter half of the 3rd century and is the oldest of the group. It has a total length of 44 meters. The anterior rectangular portion is 13 meters wide and 1.6 meters high, and the posterior circular portion is 21 meters in diameter and two meters high. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included Glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and many pot-shaped earthenware vessels were unearthed from around the Kofun. <ref name= "Zentsuji1">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/digi-m-culture-detail-008-index.htmll|title=善通寺市デジタルミュージアム 野田院古墳 |work=Zentsuji Digital Museum Nota-no-in Kofun|publisher=Zentsuji City|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2022|qq=}}</ref> The '''Ohakayama Kofun''' is located in the central part of the cluster.It is orientated to the southwest, and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. It has a side-entry stone burial chamber, and many grave goods were excavated, including the first gilt-bronze crowns found in Japan, earthenware, ornaments, weapons and horse harnesses. From these grave goods, the date of the tumulus was determined to be the late Kofun period, towards the end of the 6th century. The tumulus has a total length of 46 meters, with the anterior rectangular portion 28 meters wide and five meters high, and the posterior circular portion 28 meters in diameter and six meters high. The southern part of the anterior portion has been leveled. The burial chamber is about three meters long and about 1.8 meters wide, with a 4.5 meter entry passage. Archaeological excavation surveys have been carried out in 1982 and 1986-1990.<ref name= "Zentsuji2">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/digi-m-culture-detail-008-index.htmll|title=善通寺市デジタルミュージアム 王墓山古墳 |work=Zentsuji Digital Museum Ohakayama Kofun|publisher=Zentsuji City|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2022|qq=}}</ref> The '''Miyagao Kofun''' is a [[decorated kofun]],and retains remnants of line paintings depicting warriors on the road, groups of people on horseback, and fleets of ships on the back wall of its tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. It is estimated to date from the latter half of the 6th century. It is located west of the Ohakayama Kofun, and a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} occupies part of the mound.<ref name= "Zentsuji3">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/digi-m-culture-detail-009-index.htmll|title=善通寺市デジタルミュージアム 宮が尾古墳 |work=Zentsuji Digital Museum Miyagao Kofun|publisher=Zentsuji CIty|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2022|qq=}}</ref> The '''Murusuyama Kofun''' is located on Mt. Murusu at an elevation of 121 meters. A ship-shaped hollowed-out stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was excavated and has bene designated a National Important Cultural Property.<ref name= "Bunka2">{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10105|title=割竹形石棺 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2021|qq=}}</ref> No excavations have been carried out so far, except for the fact that it was subjected to reckless digging during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. It is orientated to the west and both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa'' have been found on the surface of the mound. There is no trace of a moat. It has a total length of 49.2 meters, with an anterior rectangular portion 10.8 meters wide and 1.75 meters high, and the posterior circular portion 42 meters in diameter and 3.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of hornblende {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} and measures 2.37 meters, 0.79 meters wide, and 0.35-0.43 meters high. The lid has a cross-section that is almost triangular with steps on the sides, and the coffin body has ridges on the sides. In addition, stone pillows were created inside the coffin, and earrings in the form of ''[[magatama]]''-shaped beads were engraved on the left and right sides of the neck, and traces of [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]] were found on the inside. This tumulus is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century, or the early Kofun period. .<ref name= "Zentsuji4">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/digi-m-culture-detail-084-index.htmll|title=善通寺市デジタルミュージアム 磨臼山古墳 |work=Zentsuji Digital Museum Murusuyama Kofun|publisher=Zentsuji City|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2022|qq=}}</ref> The '''Tsurugamine No. 4 Kofun''' is located at the southwestern end of the ridge of Tsurugamine at an elevation of 125meters. It is not covered by the National Historic Site designation. The '''Maruyama Kofun''' is located behind the main building of Kitamuki Hachiman Shrine. It is 54 meters long and 30 meters in diameter, and is thought to have been built in the middleof the 5th century. It is also not covered by the National Historic Site designation. The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Zentsuji Station|Zentsuji Station]] by the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Dosan Line|en|Dosan Line|qid=Q861077|lt=Dosan Line|1=Dosan Line}}. It also contains the Zentsuji Municipal Folk Museum, which houses and displays artifacts excavated from the Arioka Kofuns. April 29th is designated as 'Kofun Day' in Zentsuji City. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Arioka Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |{{nihongo|'''Nota-no-in Kofun'''|野田院古墳||qq=}} ||{{coords|34|11|52.1|N|133|46|46.4|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|qq=}}||keyhole||length: 44 m||late 3rd C |- |{{nihongo|'''Ohakayama Kofun'''|王墓山古墳||qq=}} ||{{coords|34|12|50.9|N|133|46|23.3|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||qq=}}||keyhole||length: 46 m ||early rth C |- |{{nihongo|'''Miyagao Kofun'''|宮が尾古墳||qq=}}||{{coords|34|12|22.9|N|133|46|8.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|qq=}}||circular|| ||early 7th C |- |{{nihongo|'''Murusuyama Kofun'''|磨臼山古墳||qq=}}||{{coords|34|13|4.8|N|133|47|35.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|qq=}}||keyhole||length: 42 m||late 4th C |} ==Gallery== <gallery> Ooasayama07.jpg| Nota-no-in Kofun 王墓山古墳 (善通寺市) 出土 金銅製冠帽・銀象嵌入鉄刀.JPG|Ohakayama Kofun excavated artifacts </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|qid=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Ohakayama Kofun (Zentsuji)|qq=}} *[https://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/ariokaindex.html Zentsuji City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
